title: Create M7 planning package and task DAG
summary: Create the reviewed M7 Early Access / public playtest preparation planning package from the verified WM-0097 future M7 entry prompt, including startup audit evidence, M7 task DAG and concrete task packets without starting M7 implementation.
acceptance: Records startup audit evidence confirming M0-M6 are done, WM-0097 is done/verified, main and origin/main are synchronized, inbox is 0, worktree is clean, only the main worktree is present, no WM-0098+ task existed before this task and M7 was not already started
Adopts coordination/reports/WM-0097-future-m7-entry-prompt.md and current Roadmap authority without redefining M7 away from Early Access / public playtest preparation

Creates a reviewed M7 task DAG covering tutorial/onboarding, early-game balance/readability, explainability UX copy, cultural review, privacy/feedback/diagnostics, Windows controlled external test instructions, Web demo-only scope, store/public playtest material draft, save compatibility policy, known issues/release notes, playtest checklist, M7 closeout and future M8 prompt

Creates concrete M7 task JSON packets with milestone, ownerRole, reviewerRole, dependencies, allowed paths, forbidden paths, branch, acceptance, validation commands, risks, owner gates, M0-M6 regression scope, file ownership, benchmark impact, rollback model and Spark eligibility
Keeps downstream M7 packets proposed until WM-0098 is independently reviewed, integrated and marked done, then normal taskctl dependency promotion may unlock ready tasks
Does not implement product runtime behavior, create public release artifacts, submit stores, sign builds, enable telemetry/accounts/paid services, make final privacy/legal/store claims or start M8

history.details: Created from the verified WM-0097 future M7 entry prompt after owner started the M7 Early Access / public playtest preparation goal.
WM-0097 is done, reviewed, integrated and pushed; WM-0098 is the first planning-only M7 task.

Created M7 planning-only package, DAG and proposed WM-0099 through WM-0110 task packets from verified WM-0097 future M7 prompt; updated roadmap/project-state planning markers; no M7 implementation, public release, owner-gated action or M8 work started; required gates passed with isolated bench rerun passing after one recorded non-acceptance slow-sample failure.

verified: No blocking findings; Roadmap authority preserved; downstream M7 packets WM-0099 through WM-0110 are proposed/unclaimed, dependency-valid and acyclic; no product/release/M8 work started; required review checks passed.

Integrated verified M7 planning package after reviewer Gate found no issues. Integration gates passed: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check and pnpm quality; earlier full WM-0098 gates passed including ci:local, m5-invariants, M5 100000-tick headless and isolated bench rerun with nonblocking map-dirty warning.

objective: Create the formal M7 planning package and task DAG from the verified M7 entry prompt without starting M7 implementation.
forbiddenPaths: telemetry account paid service or crash upload implementation
final privacy legal or store claims
M8 task creation, promotion, claim, implementation or review
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: M7 planning becomes public release or M8 execution; owner must keep all downstream tasks scoped to preparation only.
Risk: task DAG under-covers privacy, cultural review, save compatibility, known issues or playtest materials; reviewer must compare against the M7 entry prompt and owner goal.
Risk: downstream packets are too broad or overlap file ownership; project-director must split tasks into independently reviewable slices with non-overlapping files.
File ownership is limited to M7 planning reports, concrete M7 task packets and narrow roadmap/project-state markers.
ownerGate: Block if M7 scope cannot be uniquely planned from WM-0097 future M7 prompt and current Roadmap

Do not execute public release, Early Access release, store submission, Web public launch, signed Windows installer, telemetry, account system, paid service, crash upload or final privacy/legal/store/save-compatibility commitments
Do not change Web verdict away from demo-only or Windows verdict beyond controlled external test without owner approval
Do not create, promote, claim, implement or review M8
Preserve M0-M6 regression gates and benchmark thresholds
m0M6RegressionCoverage: Preserve all closed task states and review history through WM-0097
Preserve M5 final world/read-model hashes 0xfba70a5c / 0x9ba83cb7 unless a reviewed migration changes them
Preserve M6 Web demo-only verdict and Windows unsigned controlled-external-test verdict
Keep Simulation Worker or Node headless as the only authoritative world writer
benchmarkImpact: No product benchmark baseline or artifact changes. WM-0098 records M7 benchmark requirements only and preserves the 10 percent warning / 20 percent blocking thresholds.
reviewRouting: Project-director completes to reviewer. Reviewer must verify that downstream M7 packets preserve the reviewed M7 entry prompt scope, dependencies, owner gates and non-goals before WM-0098 can be integrated.
rollbackModel: Revert WM-0098 task/report files and the newly created downstream M7 task JSON packets; leave WM-0097 verified closeout and future M7 prompt intact and M7 implementation unstarted.

sparkEligibility.reason: M7 milestone planning, product gate scope, task DAG design, owner gates and final review cannot be assigned to Spark.

review.summary: No blocking findings; Roadmap authority preserved; downstream M7 packets WM-0099 through WM-0110 are proposed/unclaimed, dependency-valid and acyclic; no product/release/M8 work started; required review checks passed.

integration.summary: Integrated verified M7 planning package after reviewer Gate found no issues. Integration gates passed: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check and pnpm quality; earlier full WM-0098 gates passed including ci:local, m5-invariants, M5 100000-tick headless and isolated bench rerun with nonblocking map-dirty warning.
